functions.name: queryflux_execute_query
functions.description: Execute SQL query on a connected database using QueryFlux. Supports SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and other SQL statements. Can be run in dry-run mode for validation without execution.

sql.description: SQL query to execute. Can be any valid SQL statement (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, etc.)

dry_run.description: If true, validates the query without executing it. Useful for testing query syntax. Default: false

functions.name: queryflux_get_schema
functions.description: Get complete database schema including all tables, columns, data types, primary keys, foreign keys, and indexes. Essential for understanding database structure before writing queries.

functions.name: queryflux_natural_language_query
functions.description: Convert a natural language question into an optimized SQL query using AI. The AI understands the database schema and generates accurate, efficient SQL. Returns the SQL query with a confidence score and explanation.

question.description: Natural language question about the data (e.g., 'Show me all users who signed up last week', 'What are the top 10 products by revenue?')

functions.name: queryflux_create_migration
functions.description: Generate SQL migration scripts (both up and down) from a natural language description of database changes. Includes safety validation to prevent data loss. Returns idempotent DDL statements.

description.description: Natural language description of the migration (e.g., 'Add index on users.email for faster lookups', 'Add a status column to orders table', 'Create a payments table')

validate.description: If true, runs safety validation to check for destructive operations. Default: true

functions.name: queryflux_seed_test_data
functions.description: Generate realistic test data for database tables using AI. Can create human-like names, emails, addresses, or random data. Useful for development, testing, and demos. Dry-run by default for safety.

table_name.description: Name of the table to seed data into

row_count.description: Number of rows to generate (between 1 and 1000)

data_type.description: Type of data to generate: 'realistic' for human-like data (names, emails, addresses) or 'random' for UUIDs and random strings

execute.description: If true, inserts the data into the database. If false (default), returns the SQL preview only. Default: false

functions.name: queryflux_explain_query
functions.description: Analyze query execution plan and provide optimization suggestions. Uses PostgreSQL EXPLAIN ANALYZE to identify slow operations, missing indexes, and inefficient joins. Returns actionable recommendations prioritized by impact.

query.description: SQL query to analyze for performance optimization

analyze.description: If true, runs EXPLAIN ANALYZE (executes query and shows actual timing). If false, runs EXPLAIN only (estimates without execution). Default: true
